source.page_label: Hunting Upland Game and Turkey | Utah DWR
source.update_note: 2026-27 season data compiled from the Utah DWR Upland Game page (last updated June 26, 2026) and the 2025-26 Utah Upland Game and Turkey Guidebook. Where 2026-27 dates were unavailable, 2025-26 dates from the guidebook and Project Upland reference were used.

species.name: Sharp-tailed Grouse
species.hunting_units: Box Elder and Cache counties only

species.name: Greater Sage Grouse
species.hunting_units: Diamond/Blue Mountain, Parker Mountain, Rich County, West Box Elder areas

species.name: California (Valley) Quail
species.hunting_units: Statewide (except Washington County for Gambel's range)

species.name: Gray (Hungarian) Partridge
species.hunting_units: Box Elder and Cache counties primarily

licenses.name: Basic Hunting License (Resident, age 18-64)
licenses.covers: Required for all resident hunters; 365-day license

licenses.name: Basic Hunting License (Nonresident, age 18+)
licenses.covers: Required for all nonresident hunters; 365-day license

licenses.name: Nonresident 3-Day Small Game License
licenses.covers: Small game and upland game birds for 3 consecutive days

licenses.name: Sage Grouse / Sharp-tailed Grouse Permit
licenses.covers: Required to hunt sage grouse or sharp-tailed grouse

licenses.name: White-tailed Ptarmigan / Band-tailed Pigeon Permit
licenses.covers: Free permit required to hunt ptarmigan or band-tailed pigeon

key_notes.title: A valid hunting license is required for all hunters. Credit/debit card transactions incur a 2.2% fee.

sources.label: Utah DWR — Fees Page
sources.evidence: A 2.2% transaction fee applies for all online and in-person credit/debit card transactions.

key_notes.title: Sage grouse and sharp-tailed grouse require a $10 permit (resident) in addition to a hunting license. Seasons are limited to approximately 3 weeks in fall.

sources.label: Project Upland — Utah Bird Hunting Guide
sources.evidence: Sharp-tailed Grouse: Permit required ($10). Sage Grouse: Permit required ($10). Both: 2 birds per season, Sept 27 - Oct 19.

key_notes.title: White-tailed ptarmigan requires a free permit in addition to a hunting license. Found only in the Uinta Mountains.

sources.label: Utah DWR — Ptarmigan Information
sources.evidence: White-tailed Ptarmigan: Free permit required. Season Sept 1 - Oct 31. Uinta Mountains only.

key_notes.title: Scaled (blue) quail are closed to hunting in Utah. Hunters must be able to distinguish them from Gambel's and California quail.

sources.label: Utah DWR — Upland Game Page
sources.evidence: Scaled (Blue) Quail: Closed statewide. Hunters must be able to identify them.

key_notes.title: Only rooster (male) pheasants may be taken. Youth pheasant days run before the regular season.

sources.label: Project Upland — Utah Bird Hunting Guide
sources.evidence: Ring-necked Pheasant: 2/6 (roosters only). Youth Pheasant Days: Oct 25-30.

key_notes.title: Youth quail and partridge days run in late October before the regular November 1 opener.

sources.label: Project Upland — Utah Bird Hunting Guide
sources.evidence: Youth Quail Days: Oct 25-27. Youth Chukar/Hun: Sept 20-22.
